name: Early-Stage Startup Pitch
summary: Seed and pre-seed pitch arc tuned for vision-heavy stories where revenue and traction are still thin. Anchors the narrative in market change, founder insight, and credible early signal — not metrics yet.

beats.name: Logo
beats.description: Cover slide with the company name, logo, and one-line positioning. Simple, branded, confident.

beats.name: Monumental Change
beats.description: Open by naming a large, undeniable shift in the world. Tech, regulatory, behavioral. The shift is the reason this company exists — investors should feel its weight before they hear about the product.

beats.name: Past Struggle
beats.description: Show what the world tried to do about the problem before the shift, and why those approaches fell short. Honest credit to predecessors makes the new game more credible.

beats.name: Innovative Breakthrough
beats.description: Present the founding insight or breakthrough that makes a new approach possible. This is the 'secret' the company is built on — name it plainly.

beats.name: Unique Offering
beats.description: Detail the product or service. Lead with the customer-visible thing they touch; the architecture can wait. Show why this offering is structurally different from the past struggle.

beats.name: Costly Issues
beats.description: Quantify the pain customers experience without your offering. Time, dollars, opportunity cost. Specific numbers convert sympathy into urgency.

beats.name: Effective Solutions
beats.description: Demonstrate the customer outcome your offering produces. Before/after numbers from early users, even small N. Early-stage pitches are won by leading indicators, not by trailing ones.

beats.name: Unit Economics
beats.description: Explain the financial model. Cost to acquire, gross margin, payback. Investors don't expect maturity — they expect that you've thought about it.

beats.name: Talented Team
beats.description: Introduce the founders and key early hires. Show why this team uniquely sees and can execute on this opportunity. Insight beats résumé at the seed stage.

beats.name: Traction
beats.description: Showcase the early signal: pilots, design partners, waitlist, retention data, anything credible. Frame it honestly — investors at this stage discount inflation aggressively.

beats.name: Investment Opportunity
beats.description: Detail the round size, structure, and use of funds. Tie the raise to specific milestones it takes the company to. Specificity here signals operational maturity.

beats.name: Closing Logo
beats.description: Close on the brand and contact info. Leave the room with the line you most want repeated when the partner pitches you to the rest of the firm.
